message.content:
Test file: src/claim-office.spec.ts
Implementation file: src/claim-office.ts
Failing test: "cursed sword (steel, enchantment 3) for newcomer yields 165 G"
Current error: expected 115, want 165
Passing tests so far: 11

Spec: Cursed items add 50% risk surcharge on the item's base premium.
Calculation: 100 base + 50 (curse 50% of base) + 10 (first insurance, 10% of base) + 5 fee = 165.

Add cursed handling in itemPremium: if item.cursed === true, add 50% of base premium.

Keep first-insurance applied to the item's base premium (10%), the same way it works now.

Do NOT refactor.
EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, return after completion.

content.text: I'll analyze the failing test and implement minimal code to make it pass.

content.text:
Green Phase Complete.

**Implementation**: Added a `cursedSurcharge` line in `itemPremium` that adds 50% of the item's base premium when `item.cursed === true`, otherwise 0. The surcharge is added before the first-insurance surcharge (which remains 10% of base premium only, per the spec).

**Result**: All 12 tests now pass (was 11).

**Approach**: This is minimal because:
- It uses a simple ternary check on `item.cursed === true`
- It only modifies the `itemPremium` function (the spec said curse adds to the item's base premium)
- It doesn't touch component logic (cursed items aren't components in any test)
- It preserves the existing first-insurance calculation exactly as it was (10% of base premium, not including the curse surcharge), matching the spec's calculation: 100 + 50 + 10 + 5 = 165

No refactoring performed.

Relevant file: /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-17_04-49-14_claim-office-example-mapping_v7-hybrid-green-refactor_opus-4-7-no-thinking/src/claim-office.ts
